figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) We consider lattices ofN = Nx×Ny (Ny = 2 here) spin-1/2 particles interacting with direction-dependent nearest neighbor couplings J∥, J⊥. (b),(c) Rung magnetization dynamics mz(x, t) = ∑ y ⟨Ŝ z (x,y)(t)⟩ from an initial state with a single ferromagnetic domain wall. (b) Decoupled chains (J⊥ = 0) map to free fermions and show undamped ballistic spin motion. (c) Nonzero J⊥ leads to non-thermalizing non-homogeneous magnetization profiles. (d) Long-time average rung magnetization, mz(x), showing stronger domains as J⊥/J∥ increases. (e) Long-time average of the magnetization at the left edge,mz(x = 0), vs even Nx with Ny = 2. The initial state has total magnetization Sz = 0 with two equally sized ferromagnetic domains. For large even Nx, m z(x = 0) approaches a nonzero constant if J⊥ ≳ 0.5J∥. See Stability of non-ergodic dynamics and Localization transition for details.
